title: Recovery Verifier Primitive v1
description: Schema candidate for P29 RecoveryVerifier — enforces K10 (RECOVERY): re-engagement after halt requires root-cause verification, evidence collection, and operator approval. Gates the kill_switch/circuit_breaker recovery path. Status: PROPOSED — not yet implemented.

halt_event_id.description: ID of the halt event that triggered the recovery process.

identified.description: Whether root cause has been identified.

fix_applied.description: Whether a fix has been applied to address the root cause.

root_cause_analysis.description: Root cause analysis gate: must identify and address the cause before re-engagement.

evidence_refs.description: References to evidence: logs, test results, fix verification.

test_results_ref.description: Reference to test results verifying the fix.

health_check_ref.description: Reference to health probe results post-fix.

evidence.description: Evidence gate: proof that root cause is addressed.

approver_id.description: Identity of the operator approving re-engagement.

conditions.description: Conditions under which re-engagement is approved.

operator_approval.description: Operator approval gate: re-engagement requires explicit human approval.

receipt.description: Receipt gate: hash-chained proof of recovery verification.

strategy.description: Re-engagement strategy: immediate full restart, gradual ramp-up, canary test first, or manual step-by-step.

monitoring_duration_minutes.description: How long to monitor after re-engagement before considering recovery complete.

rollback_on_failure.description: Whether to auto-rollback if re-engagement fails.

re_engagement_plan.description: Re-engagement plan: how the system will be brought back online.
